bull.b: Cibus reports major platform gains—an order‑of‑magnitude boost in rice editing efficiency and single‑cell regeneration in wheat aided by AI/ML. These durable technical improvements should shorten development cycles, raise throughput and lower per‑trait costs, strengthening long‑term licensing and commercialization scal

The company has accumulated meaningful regulatory wins (17 positive USDA‑APHIS determinations plus Ecuador/Peru equivalence and progress on EU NGT rules). Structural regulatory clarity reduces commercialization friction and partner uncertainty, improving the durable likelihood of cross‑jurisdiction launches and recurri

Cibus has advanced concrete commercial steps in LATAM rice—an Interoc LOI, import permit, delivery of edited materials, and seven active seed‑company relationships targeting a 2027 initial launch. These durable commercial milestones, if converted, can establish recurring royalty streams and validate the company’s go‑to

bear.b: Cibus is burning cash at a high absolute rate relative to its revenue base (TTM cash flow ≈ -$50M) and reports roughly $30M of cash, giving runway into late Q1 2027 per management. This persistent burn creates structural funding risk, likely necessitating further raises or sharp revenue gains, pressuring dilution and s

bear.t: Sustained Large Losses And Negative Margins
bear.b: Despite modest top‑line growth, the business remains far from profitable: revenue is tiny and margins deeply negative (gross profit slightly negative; net margin extremely depressed). This structural unprofitability limits internal funding for commercial scale‑up and keeps near‑term viability dependent on partners or e

Multiple commercial arrangements are still LOIs or early‑stage partner programs and the U.S. rice launch was pushed to 2029 due to herbicide registration timing.
